summary: The commenter, an individual attorney, strongly opposes the HTI-5 proposed deregulatory actions that would remove or weaken audit trail and privacy/security certification criteria. He argues that audit trails are essential for patient safety, data integrity, and accountability, and urges ONC to retain these safeguards until modernized standards are established.
key_arguments: Audit trails are foundational for trust, transparency, and accountability in electronic health information.
Removing audit requirements reduces the ability to detect errors, prevent harm, and investigate fraud.
Deregulation should not precede the establishment of updated standards for data creation, preservation, and exchange.
Weakening privacy and security safeguards shifts risk onto patients and erodes public confidence.
recommendations: Retain and reinforce audit trail certification criteria until modernized standards are codified.
Update definitions and standards for audit logs, tamper resistance, and traceability to ensure consistent expectations.
Do not remove privacy and security safeguards until robust mechanisms protect data integrity and patient access.
Avoid deregulation that undermines tools for identifying systemic risk and protecting patients.
notable_quote: An audit trail is not an optional feature. It is the foundation of trust in electronic health information.
